Title: CELLULOSE SYNTHASE-LIKE C proteins modulate cell wall establishment during ethylene-mediated root growth inhibition in rice

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide that restricts cell wall extension and cell elongation during ethylene/auxin responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root epidermal cell wall, especially elongation and differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xyloglucan biosynthesis / primary cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Xyloglucan glucan synthase / cellulose synthase-like C protein
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like C (CSLC) family glycosyltransferase; D,D,D,QQHRW motif; six transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the beta-1,4-glucan backbone of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Xyloglucan beta-1,4-glucan backbone / xyloglucan-derived oligosaccharides after enzymatic digestion
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core polymer backbone of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene- and auxin-induced xyloglucan accumulation and inhibits root elongation; overexpression causes shorter roots and ethylene/NAA hypersensitivity, whereas mutation reduces ethylene sensitivity and XyG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme for ethylene/auxin-induced XyG accumulation in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; higher-order mutant analysis; PACE; UPLC-MS; immunodetection with LM15; subcellular localization; genetic interaction with Oseil1 and Ostar2; xyloglucanase rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ethylene- and auxin-inducible in rice root tips
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in root tips by ethylene and NAA; associated with root elongation/differentiation zone response
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethylene treatment; auxin (NAA) treatment; dark-grown etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsEIL1; OsIAA1; OsIAA9; OsIAA21; OsIAA31; OsARF4; OsARF7; OsARF8; OsARF23
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCSLC2 is strongly induced by ethylene in an OsEIL1- and auxin-dependent manner. 2. OsCSLC2 overexpression increases XyG accumulation and shortens roots, while oscslc2 mutants show reduced ethylene sensitivity and reduced XyG accumulation. 3. OsCSLC2 acts downstream of OsEIL1 and OsTAR2 to promote XyG deposition in root epidermal cell walls and restrict cell elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: oscslc2 and especially higher-order oscslc mutants show constitutive short roots and broader growth defects; higher-order mutants have altered seed traits, reduced plant height, smaller panicles, lower seed setting and yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering root growth plasticity and stress adaptation via cell wall remodeling, but manipulation must balance strong developmental/yield penalties and may require tissue- or condition-specific tuning
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native OsCSLC2 promoter in LUC assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Xyloglucanase treatment alleviated the short-root phenotype of OsEIL1-OE, supporting XyG as the causal wall component downstream of OsCSLC2

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide restricting cell elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root cell wall, especially epidermal wall in elongation/differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xyloglucan biosynthesis / primary cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Xyloglucan glucan synthase / cellulose synthase-like C protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes the beta-1,4-glucan backbone of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core polymer backbone of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with OsCSLC2/7/9 in promoting ethylene-mediated root growth inhibition via XyG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; part of a redundant key enzyme set
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; promoter-LUC; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ethylene inducible in root tips in an OsEIL1-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCSLC1 is induced by ethylene in rice roots. 2. Its induction depends on OsEIL1 and OsEIL1 directly activates its promoter. 3. It contributes redundantly with other OsCSLCs to ethylene-induced XyG accumulation and root growth inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order cslc mutants including oscslc1239 show severe developmental defects and yield penalties
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacked target with other CSLC genes for tuning root growth responses, but redundancy and pleiotropy require caution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not individually mutant-characterized in isolation in this study; inferred function mainly from redundancy and direct transcriptional activation by OsEIL1

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide restricting cell elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root cell wall, especially epidermal wall in elongation/differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xyloglucan biosynthesis / primary cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Xyloglucan glucan synthase / cellulose synthase-like C protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes the beta-1,4-glucan backbone of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core polymer backbone of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redundantly promotes ethylene-induced XyG synthesis and root growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; part of a redundant key enzyme set
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; promoter-LUC; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ethylene inducible in root tips in an OsEIL1-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCSLC7 is induced by ethylene in an OsEIL1-dependent manner. 2. OsEIL1 directly activates the OsCSLC7 promoter. 3. OsCSLC7 contributes redundantly with OsCSLC1/2/9 to XyG-mediated inhibition of root elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of higher-order mutant combinations causing strong growth and yield defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for combinatorial engineering of wall-mediated root plasticity, but high redundancy and developmental pleiotropy are key cautions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role supported mainly through transcriptional regulation and higher-order mutant phenotypes

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide restricting cell elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root cell wall, especially epidermal wall in elongation/differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xyloglucan biosynthesis / primary cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Xyloglucan glucan synthase / cellulose synthase-like C protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes the beta-1,4-glucan backbone of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core polymer backbone of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redundantly promotes ethylene-induced XyG synthesis and root growth inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; part of a redundant key enzyme set
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; RT-qPCR; promoter-LUC; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ethylene inducible in root tips in an OsEIL1-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCSLC9 is induced by ethylene in roots. 2. Its induction depends on OsEIL1, which directly activates its promoter. 3. OsCSLC9 functions redundantly with other OsCSLCs in XyG-mediated inhibition of root elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of higher-order mutant combinations showing severe growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target for modifying root wall mechanics, with caution due to pleiotropic effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in oscslc1239 quadruple mutant that showed strong defects and reduced XyG levels

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xyloglucan biosynthesis / primary cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Xyloglucan glucan synthase / cellulose synthase-like C protein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes the beta-1,4-glucan backbone of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core polymer backbone of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ethylene-responsive CSLC homolog likely contributing to XyG biosynthesis, but direct activation by OsEIL1 was not supported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ethylene inducible in root tips in an OsEIL1-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCSLC10 is induced by ethylene in an OsEIL1-dependent manner. 2. Unlike OsCSLC1/2/7/9, OsEIL1 did not activate the OsCSLC10 promoter in protoplast assays. 3. It may be indirectly promoted in the ethylene response and contribute to the broader XyG pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for pathway-level engineering of root wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulation appears indirect relative to OsCSLC1/2/7/9

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide restricting root cell elongation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root epidermal cell wall in elongation and differentiation zone
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Xyloglucan biosynthesis and cell wall establishment during ethylene-mediated root growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsCSLC1, OsCSLC2, OsCSLC7 and OsCSLC9 transcription; indirectly promotes OsCSLC10 and broader XyG metabolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master ethylene-response regulator required for most ethylene-induced XyG accumulation in roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for ethylene-induced XyG accumulation, cell wall thickening, and root growth inhibition; oseil1 mutants are ethylene-insensitive in root length
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; overexpression background analysis; RT-qPCR; promoter-LUC transactivation; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; PACE; UPLC-MS; immunodetection
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in auxin-linked positive feedback described previously via OsEIL1-OsIAA module; here integrates ethylene with auxin to promote XyG pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Acts in rice root tips during ethylene response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Required in root tips for ethylene induction of XyG pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Auxin promotes OsEIL1 activity in a feedback loop through OsEIL1-OsIAA1/9/21/31 module-mediated histone acetylation, based on previous study cited and incorporated here
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation abolishes ethylene-mediated root growth inhibition; pathway perturbation likely affects broad hormone signaling outputs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value master regulator for tuning root growth plasticity, but broad ethylene/auxin signaling roles imply pleiotropic risks
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S/constitutive promoter in overexpression lines; native target promoters in LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsEIL1 is essential for ethylene-induced XyG accumulation and root growth inhibition. 2. OsEIL1 directly binds and activates OsCSLC1, OsCSLC2, OsCSLC7 and OsCSLC9 promoters. 3. OsCSLC2 acts genetically downstream of OsEIL1, linking ethylene signaling to cell wall biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsEIL1 also controls auxin biosynthesis gene OsTAR2 from prior work and broadly regulates XyG-related XXT and XTH genes in this study

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide restricting root elongation downstream of hormone signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Auxin biosynthesis enzyme functioning as pathway regulator in signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin-dependent promotion of XyG biosynthesis pathway during ethylene response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes auxin biosynthesis required for transcriptional induction of OsCSLC2 and other XyG-pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsCSLC2; other OsCSLC homologs; OsXXT1; OsGT3-OsGT7; XTH genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for normal ethylene induction of OsCSLC2 and multiple XyG metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential auxin-pathway input for ethylene-induced XyG accumulation in rice roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ostar2 mutants have reduced XyG accumulation and lose normal ethylene-induced increases in xylose/cellulose and XyG, causing ethylene-insensitive root growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; genetic interaction with OsCSLC2-OE; RT-qPCR; monosaccharide/cellulose analysis; PACE; UPLC-MS
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene via direct activation by OsEIL1 (from previous study referenced here)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of ethylene-auxin positive loop described previously
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Acts in root tips during ethylene response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Required in roots for ethylene-triggered XyG pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation disrupts normal root hormonal response; broader auxin-related developmental effects likely
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible leverage point for hormone-dependent root wall remodeling, but broad auxin pathway pleiotropy is a major constraint
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTAR2 is required for ethylene-induced increases in xylose, cellulose and XyG accumulation in roots. 2. Ethylene induction of OsCSLC2 and other XyG-pathway genes is impaired in ostar2. 3. OsCSLC2 acts genetically downstream of OsTAR2 in the root ethylene response.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, it is treated here as a regulatory core gene because its primary role in this paper is as auxin-pathway input controlling downstream wall-metabolism genes rather than as a direct XyG biosynthetic enzyme

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsCSLC2 promoter activity in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to auxin-dependent activation of OsCSLC2 and thus XyG synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-LUC assay; expression induction by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Substantially induced by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for fine-tuning auxin-dependent cell wall remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsCSLC2 native promoter in LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsARF4 activates the OsCSLC2 promoter in protoplasts. 2. OsARF4 expression is induced by ethylene and auxin. 3. It is a candidate auxin-pathway regulator contributing to OsCSLC2-mediated XyG accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No mutant phenotyping provided in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsCSLC2 promoter activity in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to auxin-dependent activation of OsCSLC2 and XyG synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-LUC assay; expression induction by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Substantially induced by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for tuning auxin/ethylene control of root wall remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsCSLC2 native promoter in LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsARF7 activates the OsCSLC2 promoter. 2. OsARF7 is induced by ethylene and auxin. 3. It likely contributes to auxin-mediated promotion of XyG biosynthesis in roots.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct in planta mutant validation in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsCSLC2 promoter activity in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to auxin-dependent activation of OsCSLC2 and XyG synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-LUC assay; expression induction by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Substantially induced by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for fine-tuning OsCSLC2-dependent root wall responses
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsCSLC2 native promoter in LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsARF8 activates the OsCSLC2 promoter. 2. OsARF8 expression is induced by ethylene and auxin. 3. It likely participates in auxin-mediated activation of the XyG pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence limited to transactivation and expression response in this study

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsCSLC2 promoter activity in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to auxin-dependent activation of OsCSLC2 and XyG synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-LUC assay; expression induction by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Substantially induced by ethylene and auxin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering auxin-sensitive wall remodeling in roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsCSLC2 native promoter in LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsARF23 activates the OsCSLC2 promoter. 2. OsARF23 is induced by ethylene and auxin. 3. It likely helps connect auxin signaling to OsCSLC2-driven XyG accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No mutant validation in this paper
